title: 1553rd meeting (9-11 March 2026) (DH) - H46-31 Lingurar v. Romania (Application No. 48474/14)
text: 3 CM/Del/Dec(2025)12../… MINISTERS’ DEPUTIES Decisions CM/Del/Dec(2026)1553/H46-31 11 March 2026 1553rd meeting, 9-11 March 2026 (DH) H46-31 Lingurar v. Romania (Application No. 48474/14) Supervision of the execution of the European Court’s judgments Reference document CM/Notes/1553/H46-31 Decisions The Deputies 1. recalled that this case concerns the ill-treatment suffered by the applicants during a police raid based on discriminatory ethnic profiling of a Roma community The terms “Roma and Travellers” are being used at the Council of Europe to encompass the wide diversity of the groups covered by the work of the Council of Europe in this field: on the one hand a) Roma, Sinti/Manush, Calé, Kaale, Romanichals, Boyash/Rudari; b) Balkan Egyptians (Egyptians and Ashkali); c) Eastern groups (Dom, Lom and Abdal); and, on the other hand, groups such as Travellers, Yenish, and the populations designated under the administrative term “Gens du voyage”, as well as persons who identify themselves as Gypsies. and the relevant authorities’ failure to conduct effective investigations and proceedings including into the applicants’ allegations of racist motives for the raid and for their ill-treatment and thus to meet the higher standard of response imposed by the Convention “in situations where there is evidence of patterns of violence and intolerance against an ethnic minority”; As regards general measures 2. noted positively the legislation adopted to strengthen general safeguards against discrimination and hate speech; invited the authorities to provide further clarification on how these provisions operate together to prevent, detect and sanction discrimination, ethnic or racial profiling and hate crime; encouraged them to consider including an explicit prohibition of racial or ethnic profiling, or other alternative measures, in the relevant provisions governing the duties and responsibilities of officers who plan and oversee police operations; 3. invited the authorities to provide information on the implementation and results in the area of police operations of the National Strategy for the Prevention and Combating of Antisemitism, Xenophobia, Radicalisation and Hate Speech (2021–2023 and 2024–2027), and the 2022–2027 Roma inclusion strategy, and to assess their correlation and impact within the existing legal framework; 4. noted with satisfaction the authorities’ sustained efforts to strengthen capacity and raise awareness of the risks of ethnic profiling, anti-Roma bias and stereotyping both among law‑enforcement personnel as well as the judges and prosecutors; encouraged them to prioritise these topics, including through co-operation with relevant national bodies, such as the Ombudsperson and the National Council for Combating Discrimination, and to expand their collaboration with the Council of Europe Roma and Travellers Division, as well as to continue drawing on the Council of Europe’s expertise in training and capacity‑building; 5. noted that concerns persist regarding the adequacy of investigations and judicial review in cases involving violent incidents against Roma and alleged racist motives on the part of law-enforcement officials; urged the authorities to prioritise a review of how reports and allegations of discriminatory treatment and abuse have been dealt with by the relevant police disciplinary and internal oversight bodies, and invited them to provide further information, including statistical data, on complaints received, measures taken, and outcomes; urged them to reinforce prosecutorial guidance, training and awareness-raising among the judiciary; 6. expressed concern at persisting shortcomings in data collection on racial profiling and hate crime; called upon the authorities to ensure that ongoing reforms of statistical and ECRIS case‑management systems allow for the systematic and integrated collection of disaggregated data that: (i) distinguishes offences committed by law enforcement officials from those committed by members of the public, (ii) tracks case files from registration to outcome, and (iii) includes bias indicators and procedural results; and to report on the concrete solutions envisaged and the timeline for their implementation; 7. requested the authorities to provide information about the measures taken and envisaged to respond to the remaining questions outlined above in the form of a comprehensive action plan to be submitted no later than 30 March 2027.
